The boxer is famous for his power.
He fights with great aggression.
His first match was intense.
He landed one hundred twenty punches.
That was the starting number.
The second match came next.
He improved his performance greatly.
He increased the count by twenty five percent.
This means he added thirty more punches.
So the second total was one hundred fifty.
The third match followed soon.
He aimed to beat his previous record.
He landed thirty more than before.
This brought the third total to one hundred eighty.
We must now add them all up.
The first match gave us one hundred twenty.
The second match gave us one hundred fifty.
The third match gave us one hundred eighty.
Adding the first and second gives two hundred seventy.
Adding the third to that sum is next.
Two hundred seventy plus one hundred eighty.
The final sum is four hundred fifty.
That is the total number of punches.
He showed great consistency throughout.
His power remained a key weapon.
The crowd cheered for every hit.
The series was very exciting to watch.
Each match was harder than the last.
He pushed himself to new limits.
The numbers prove his dominance clearly.
Four hundred fifty is the answer.
No other number fits the math.
The calculation is simple and clear.
